title: W3 ASSETS.md manifest (produced-asset manifest)
description: The structured shape of the ASSETS.md manifest written by the W3 Assets node (<project>/ASSETS.md). ASSETS.md is a human-readable Markdown file authored by packages/skills/assets/SKILL.md §6b; this schema commits the structured data it carries (a manifest table + header), so the format is machine-checkable and stable for W4/W5 grounding. RELATIONSHIP: index.json = the REQUESTED slots (written by W2); ASSETS.md = the PRODUCED assets (written by W3). They are kept consistent — every generated/placeholder slot appears in both. ASSETS.md never re-declares the slot keys/dims authoritatively (index.json owns those); it records what was produced per slot + provenance. This schema describes the data model; the on-disk artifact is Markdown rendering exactly these fields.

mode.description: The mode W3 ran in. 'placeholder' is the v1 default; 'gemini' is the toggle. Even in gemini mode, individual slots may be 'placeholder' (per-slot graceful degradation).

model.description: The image model used in gemini mode (e.g. 'gemini-2.5-flash-image'). 'n/a' / omit in placeholder mode.

artStyle.description: gdd.meta.artStyle — the global style anchor fed into prompts ('placeholder' is valid).

degradedReason.description: Present only if gemini was requested but the run (or specific slots) fell back to placeholder. The one-line reason (no key, sharp missing, generation blocked, etc.). Mirrored in MEMORY.md.

slotCount.description: Slot tally — must sum to total. 'pending' counts slots W3 left for the template Preloader to placeholder-fill (a safe state, not a failure).

pending.description: Left for the Preloader to fill (no W3 file on disk).

assetsDir.description: Mirror of index.json.assetsDir — where all row paths resolve.

rows.description: One row per index.json slot W3 addressed. Empty array is valid (index.json had slots:[] — the game boots on programmatic shapes; ASSETS.md still records the empty case in notes).

slot.description: The slot KEY = the Phaser texture key (copied from index.json.slots[].slot; NEVER altered by W3).

type.description: Asset kind (from index.json). Determines the loader call (see loaderHint).

path.description: assetsDir-relative path of the file W3 wrote (e.g. 'sprites/player.png'). Empty/omitted only when status is 'pending' (no file on disk; Preloader fills it).

width.description: Produced pixel width (a single animation frame's width for type:'animation').

frames.description: For type:'animation' — the ordered frame names (from index.json). The on-disk sheet has frames.length uniform cells of width×height.

status.description: Must match the slot's status written back into index.json. 'generated' = real asset; 'placeholder' = greybox file on disk; 'pending' = no W3 file, Preloader will fill.

mode.description: How THIS slot was produced (may differ from the run mode for degraded slots).

technique.description: Provenance: e.g. 'gemini-2.5-flash-image + chroma-key + trim', 'greybox rect+label (gold)', 'flat tile (POT)', '6-frame labelled strip', 'silent wav', 'Preloader generateTexture'.

description.description: The generation prompt/intent (from index.json.slots[].description or the entity fallback).

loaderHint.description: The Phaser loader call the Preloader uses for this key, e.g. "load.image('player','sprites/player.png')" or "load.spritesheet('run','sprites/run.png',{frameWidth:64,frameHeight:64})". Advisory grounding for W4/W5; the Preloader is template-owned.

notes.description: Free-form notes: failed/oversized generations + reason, pixel-snap applied, empty-slots case, degradation, anything mirrored in MEMORY.md.
